summary: Iteration 2 testing: 37/37 backend pytest cases PASS (27 from iter-1 regression + 10 new for public-track, POD, pay-invoice, email-non-blocking). Frontend Playwright verified: public /track and /track/:awb work without auth, deep-link renders status + 11-stage progress bar + 4 timeline events, invalid AWB shows error, landing navbar-track-link present, login WHITE theme confirmed (right panel rgb(255,255,255), left panel dark navy storyboard intact), customer ShipmentDetail shows pay-now-btn + UNPAID pill, employee 'Update status' modal -> Delivered reveals receiver-name + signature-canvas inputs. Resend email integration fires asynchronously and never blocks API responses (status endpoint <2s even though Resend rejects every send with 'sandbox-only recipient' - per spec this is acceptable). Backend logs show consistent 'WARNING:flystar.email:Failed to send email for FLY... ' with no crashes / no 500s.

minor.issue: Public endpoint correctly defined BEFORE /shipments/{id} in router (verified by test_route_order_track_before_id). Works as expected.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking [data-testid=pay-now-btn] did NOT transition the shipment to paid state in the UI within 3s (payment-paid-pill remained hidden and invoice page kept showing 'Pay now' button without paid-stamp). Backend POST /api/shipments/{id}/pay works correctly (pytest TestPayInvoice all 4 cases pass). Likely a missing API call wiring, a confirm modal that needs explicit submit, or state not refetched after the call. Needs main-agent verification.

data-testid='pod-photo-input' is missing on the file input. The visible 'Capture photo' button exists but does not expose the documented testid, which will block automated coverage of the optional photo path.
Spec said invalid AWB should show 'Could not find that AWB' but app shows 'Shipment not found'. Cosmetic copy mismatch only.

integration_issues.flow: Customer Pay Now -> Invoice PAID stamp
integration_issues.issue: End-to-end UI flow does not visibly mark invoice as paid after clicking Pay Now. Backend API works in pytest; frontend wiring needs review.

issues: WHITE theme confirmed: right form panel bg = rgb(255,255,255). Left storyboard panel still dark navy as designed. Looks clean and matches spec.
Renders all 11 progress stages, status pill, route, service, type, booked timestamp and timeline. Great visual.

action_items: Fix Pay Now UI flow on ShipmentDetail/Invoice: after POST /api/shipments/{id}/pay returns 200, ShipmentDetail must refetch (or update local state) so payment-paid-pill becomes visible and pay-now-btn hides. Invoice.jsx must show paid-stamp + receipt info.
Add data-testid='pod-photo-input' to the file input that backs the 'Capture photo' button in the POD modal.
(Optional) Align public-track error copy with spec ('Could not find that AWB') or update the spec - tiny copy nit.

(Optional) Decide on Resend sandbox vs verified domain: today every status email is rejected by Resend because onboarding@resend.dev only delivers to the account owner. Backend handles this gracefully but customers receive no emails. Add a verified domain in Resend or switch sender for production.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend refactor SUCCESS: server.py is now 75 lines, routers cleanly split (auth/users/shipments/analytics). Route ordering correct - /shipments/track/{awb} is defined before /shipments/{shipment_id} so the public route resolves correctly. Verified by automated test.

_fire_email helper is correctly wrapped in try/except and uses asyncio.to_thread inside email_service.send_status_email, but the outer call in routes/shipments.py is still 'await _fire_email(...)' on the request path. This still adds the Resend HTTP round-trip latency (~300-600ms) to every status update because asyncio.to_thread blocks until Resend returns. To truly be fire-and-forget, wrap with asyncio.create_task(_fire_email(...)) instead of await. Today's status endpoint still returns in <2s but at peak load this could cause slowdowns.
shipments.py is 341 lines - getting close to the 700-line guideline but still healthy.
CORS still allow_origins=['*'] + allow_credentials=False (same as iter-1 - cookie path remains dead code, only Bearer-token works). Not a blocker.
PayInvoiceIn schema only accepts {method, note} - no amount validation. Fine for a placeholder; flag for real-gateway iteration.

POD signature_base64 / pod_photo_base64 are stored as raw base64 strings inside the Mongo doc — for delivered shipments the document size could balloon (potential >1MB if photos are large). Consider GridFS / object-storage later.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added 10 new tests: TestPublicTracking x4, TestPOD x1, TestPayInvoice x4, TestEmailNonBlocking x1)

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (Pay Now UI flow broken, pod-photo-input testid missing; all other flows green)

test_credentials: admin@flystar.in/Admin@123, employee@flystar.in/Employee@123, customer@flystar.in/Customer@123 - all verified working.
seed_data_creation: Pytest created: multiple TEST_ prefixed customers, several FLY shipments at various lifecycle stages (one paid via admin, one paid via customer, one delivered with POD, one in_transit). Frontend Playwright did not create additional shipments — only logged in / clicked.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is a full regression suite (37 tests, all green). Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. New iter-2 test classes: TestPublicTracking, TestPOD (fixture: pod_shipment), TestPayInvoice, TestEmailNonBlocking. The frontend Pay Now flow needs a retest after main agent fixes ShipmentDetail/Invoice state-refresh. POD modal also needs data-testid='pod-photo-input' on the file <input type=file>. The customer dashboard linked 5 paid+unpaid shipments — use any /portal/shipment/{id} with payment_status='unpaid' for the Pay Now retest. Resend email failures in backend logs are expected (sandbox limitation) and DO NOT indicate a bug.
rca of the issue: Pay Now UI bug: backend endpoint /api/shipments/{id}/pay returns 200 with payment_status='paid' (verified by 4 pytest cases). After UI click on [data-testid='pay-now-btn'], shipment detail still shows UNPAID pill + Pay now button and invoice page still renders the 'Pay now' header button instead of the diagonal paid-stamp. Most likely cause: ShipmentDetail.jsx either (a) does not await/refetch the shipment after POST /pay completes, or (b) is opening a confirm modal that the playwright test didn't complete. Recommended fix: after pay() promise resolves, call refetch() (or update local state with the response payload), and gate the Pay now button on 'payment_status !== "paid"' from a useState() that's updated. Same pattern needed in Invoice.jsx for the diagonal paid stamp.
